name: fablecut
description: Zero-dependency browser video editor that AI agents can drive — JSON timeline, MCP tools, live-reloading UI.
long_description: FableCut is a Premiere-style non-linear video editor that runs in the browser with zero npm dependencies. An AI agent edits video by patching a single project.json timeline (clips, keyframes, transitions, text animations, filters, speed ramps, adjustment layers) through MCP tools; the open editor UI live-reloads within ~150 ms and exports MP4 via ffmpeg. Includes reference-video analysis (shot cuts, beats, BPM, energy, drop) so agents can remake an edit with new footage.

tools.name: fablecut_status
tools.description: Auto-start the editor server and return its URL plus a project summary

tools.name: fablecut_docs
tools.description: Return the full editor manual (project.json schema, props, recipes), optionally a single section

tools.name: fablecut_get_project
tools.description: Read the timeline JSON (compact mode available for token-efficient planning)

tools.name: fablecut_set_project
tools.description: Replace the timeline JSON with optimistic-concurrency conflict detection

tools.name: fablecut_patch_project
tools.description: Apply targeted, merge-safe ops (add/update/remove clip or media, set project fields)

tools.name: fablecut_import_media
tools.description: Copy a local file into the project's media folder and register it

tools.name: fablecut_analyze_reference
tools.description: Analyze a reference video into an edit blueprint: shot cuts, beats, BPM, energy curve, drop; extracts its music
